user_query:
I need you to simulate a full run of my OpenClaw config backup workflow using the provided snapshots. Read the current config under input/openclaw_current/ and the existing backup root under input/prev_backup/. Then:
1) Set the backup directory by writing a config file at output/openclaw/backup.json with JSON: {"backup_location":"output/backup_store"}. Treat this as the changedir step.
2) Detect changes by comparing input/openclaw_current/ against the latest date-named folder under input/prev_backup/.
3) If changes are detected, create a new backup under output/backup_store/<YYYY-MM-DD>/ where the date value comes from input/backup_date.txt. Copy all files from input/openclaw_current/ except the entire workspace/ subtree. Do not modify anything under input/.
4) Append a changelog entry at output/backup_store/changelog.md headed exactly "## <YYYY-MM-DD> <HH:MM:SS>", where the time comes from input/backup_time.txt. Under that header, list one bullet per changed file in relative path form with a status in parentheses exactly as: "(modified)", "(added)", or "(deleted)".
5) Ensure workspace/ is excluded both from the backup and from the changelog.
6) Use the provided snapshots so that the changelog includes at least these three lines (order is not important):
- openclaw.json (modified)
- extensions/feishu/skills/feishu-doc/SKILL.md (added)
- agents/bot/config.yaml (deleted)
Write all outputs only under output/, creating any necessary directories.
